behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted file paths, helper function names, literal strings ('Merge without running clippy', 'CI handles this (clippy)', 'cargo clippy --all-targets', 'clippy.toml', 'too-many-lines-threshold'), and the >=10 fn requirement are explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to refactor and what files to update, but does not provide literal code/line replacements beyond the required string tokens.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on specific helper function names (resolve_provider_and_model, resolve_session_id, handle_resumed_session_workdir, resolve_and_load_extensions, configure_session_prompts) — an alternative valid decomposition using different names would fail, though instruction does specify these exact names so it's consistent with instruction.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute cargo fmt, cargo check, cargo clippy subprocesses in addition to text inspection.

test_not_tautological.reason: Cargo clippy/check subprocess tests require actual code compilation and linting to succeed; stubs wouldn't pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each instruction requirement (file deletions, clippy.toml, doc updates, helper functions, build_session calling them) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped under commented sections: fail-to-pass behavioral, config file updates, pass-to-pass CI, and static checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task doesn't produce structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and commands appear correct and consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file to refactor (builder.rs) which is legitimate task scoping, not line-level leakage.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Config edits are localized string replacements for clippy command references across specific listed files.
